name: Discount Threshold Validation
description: Enforce maximum discount percentage threshold per business policy
errorMessage: Discount cannot exceed [THRESHOLD_PERCENT]% per [POLICY_REFERENCE]. Please contact [APPROVER] for approval.

THRESHOLD_PERCENT.description: Maximum discount as percentage for error message

examples.name: Opportunity Discount Limit 15%
examples.useCase: Limit opportunity discount to 15% per sales policy
examples.errorMessage: Discount cannot exceed 15% per Sales Policy v2.3. Please contact Sales Manager for approval.

examples.name: Quote Line Discount Limit 20%
examples.useCase: Limit quote line item discount to 20%
examples.errorMessage: Line item discount cannot exceed 20%. Contact Sales Operations for approval.

examples.name: Product Discount Limit 10%
examples.errorMessage: Standard discount cannot exceed 10% per Pricing Guidelines 2025.

testCases.name: Should fail validation (discount exceeds threshold)
testCases.errorMessage: Discount cannot exceed 15% per Sales Policy v2.3.

variations.name: Tiered Discount Threshold
variations.description: Different thresholds based on opportunity amount
variations.formula:
OR(
  AND(Amount <= 50000, Discount_Percent__c > 0.10),
  AND(Amount > 50000, Amount <= 100000, Discount_Percent__c > 0.15),
  AND(Amount > 100000, Discount_Percent__c > 0.20)
)

variations.name: Record Type Specific Threshold
variations.description: Different thresholds for different record types

variations.name: Threshold with Approval Field
variations.description: Require approval field if discount exceeds threshold

bestPractices: Document threshold value in rule description with policy reference
Use decimal format in formula (0.15 not 15)
Include policy reference in error message
Consider tiered thresholds for different scenarios
Review thresholds quarterly as business changes
commonPitfalls: Using percentage format instead of decimal (15 instead of 0.15)
Not documenting policy reference (users don't know why threshold exists)
Not specifying approver in error message
Threshold too strict (no flexibility for legitimate cases)
Not reviewing threshold as business evolves

deploymentConsiderations.impactAnalysis: Query existing records with discount > threshold
deploymentConsiderations.rolloutStrategy: If many violations, consider grace period before activation
deploymentConsiderations.userCommunication: Email with policy reference and approval process
deploymentConsiderations.dataCleanup: Review high-discount opportunities, get approvals or adjust

maintenance.auditRequirements: Track all threshold exceptions with approval documentation

businessContext.industryBenchmarks: Most B2B companies: 10-20% standard discount authority

author: Salesforce Plugin Team
